title: ALLEY
blurb: Mott and Mulberry. Sanitation hasn't picked up since the eighth. Whatever's been chewing through the bags has friends now.
briefing: The garbage is stacked to the second-floor windows. Something inside it has been reproducing. You meet your first roach — not the kitchen kind; the kind you can hear from twenty feet. Bring the long gun. Keep moving.
epigraph: "A matchbook from the Hotel Carter, half-burnt. You don't open it."

debrief.win: Sanitation will do a special pickup at four. They bring two trucks. Tell nobody what was in the bags. Tell yourself even less. The Hotel Carter matchbook stays in the desk drawer.
debrief.loss: I sent Vinny down with a broom and a shopping bag. He brought back what he could find of you. Eat. Sleep. The alley will still be there tomorrow, and so will what's in it.
debrief.sGrade: Sanitation says they'll never speak of it. They will. Once. To their wives. That's how stories like this get to be stories.
